youngster.example_sentence: When I was a youngster, I loved playing soccer in the park.
youngster.original_sentence: Because you got up at 5.30 a.m. to deliver papers or milk the cows when you were a youngster doesn't necessarily mean it's a good idea for you to require your children to do the same.

not necessarily.explanation_en: used to say that something is not always true or the case
not necessarily.example_sentence: Expensive clothes are not necessarily better than cheaper ones.
not necessarily.original_sentence: Because you got up at 5.30 a.m. to deliver papers or milk the cows when you were a youngster doesn't necessarily mean it's a good idea for you to require your children to do the same.

the ultimate in.explanation_en: the best or most extreme example of something
the ultimate in.example_sentence: This new phone is the ultimate in modern technology.
the ultimate in.original_sentence: Imagine what would happen to the Ford Motor Company if its management allowed itself to think, this year we've built the ultimate in automobiles.

hereby.explanation_en: as a result of this document or action; formally
hereby.example_sentence: I hereby declare this meeting open.
hereby.original_sentence: Therefore, all experimental engineering and designing activities are hereby permanently terminated.

terminated.example_sentence: His contract was terminated early due to poor performance.
terminated.original_sentence: Therefore, all experimental engineering and designing activities are hereby permanently terminated.

mammoth.example_sentence: The company faces a mammoth task to fix its reputation.
mammoth.original_sentence: Even the mammoth Ford Motor Company would shrivel fast with this attitude.

shrivel.explanation_en: to become smaller or weaker; to wither
shrivel.example_sentence: Without enough water, the plants will soon shrivel.
shrivel.original_sentence: Even the mammoth Ford Motor Company would shrivel fast with this attitude.

undertakings.explanation_en: tasks or projects, especially ones that are important or difficult
undertakings.example_sentence: Building a new bridge is a complex undertaking.
undertakings.original_sentence: Absolute perfection in all human undertakings, from building missiles to rearing children, is unattainable.

rearing.explanation_en: the process of caring for and raising children or animals
rearing.example_sentence: She spent many years rearing her children while working part-time.
rearing.original_sentence: Absolute perfection in all human undertakings, from building missiles to rearing children, is unattainable.

unattainable.explanation_zh: 无法达到的，难以实现的
unattainable.example_sentence: Perfect health is an unattainable goal for most people.
unattainable.original_sentence: Absolute perfection in all human undertakings, from building missiles to rearing children, is unattainable.

endless room for improvement.explanation_en: there is always a way to make something better
endless room for improvement.example_sentence: Even though the essay is good, there is endless room for improvement.
endless room for improvement.original_sentence: This means there is endless room for improvement.
